source.name: Bonn University EEG Dataset (Andrzejak et al. 2001) — Reconstructed from published summary statistics
references: Andrzejak RG et al. (2001) Phys. Rev. E 64:061907
source.time_step: EEG segment index (ordered by class: A→B→C→D→E)

columns.proxy_description: EEG signal amplitude (RMS power) — neural organization/activity level.
columns.fragility_note: Amplitude depends on electrode placement and impedance. Consistent within-subject.

columns.proxy_description: Spectral entropy of EEG — signal complexity as neural resilience. High entropy = diverse frequency content = resilient state.
columns.fragility_note: Entropy measure depends on frequency resolution and window size.
columns.manipulability_note: Computed from EEG spectrum; not manipulable.

columns.proxy_description: Alpha/Beta power ratio — neural integration/coherence. High ratio indicates coordinated oscillatory activity.
columns.fragility_note: Band definitions (alpha 8-13Hz, beta 13-30Hz) are standard but arbitrary boundaries.

columns.proxy_description: High-frequency gamma power (30-100Hz) — excitability/stress on neural circuits. High gamma = high neural demand.
columns.fragility_note: Gamma band sensitive to muscle artifacts; intracranial EEG (sets C-E) avoids this.

columns.proxy_description: Cumulative spectral energy anomaly — symbolic stock of neural activation history.
columns.fragility_note: Cumulative measure; sensitive to baseline choice.
